after_payload.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Plan v0.4 of the v2 desktop UI for the CRM Builder project. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces three deliverables: ui-PRD-v0.4.md (intent, scope, acceptance criteria, error handling matrix, open questions, same shape as v0.1/v0.2/v0.3), ui-v0.4-implementation-plan.md (slice breakdown with deliverables and acceptance gates per slice), and execution prompts under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.4-{A..*}-*.md (one per implementation slice, structure matching the v0.1/v0.2/v0.3 prompts). Cadence matches v0.1/v0.2/v0.3: structured architectural discussion driven one decision at a time, building toward the PRD first, then the implementation plan, then the execution prompts."

Twelve architectural questions discussed and resolved:

Q1 (frame): v0.4's primary frame. Doug surfaced that the natural next step was preparing for a real-world test by redoing the CBM design, and CBM redo on v2 as system of record for both governance and methodology content requires v2 to have entity types for methodology content. Path chosen: (b) — CBM redo runs on v2 as system of record for both governance AND methodology content; v0.4's primary frame becomes methodology entity schema design (Bucket C from the original kickoff). Captured in DEC-038.

Q1.5 (path under b): the original kickoff explicitly forbids designing schemas inline in a planning conversation. Three paths under (b) considered: (b-α) two-stage — planning conversation pivots to workstream kickoff; (b-β) placeholder v0.4 PRD with TBD schema slots; (b-γ) scope-only inline schema design (violates kickoff constraint). Chose (b-α). Captured in DEC-038.

Q2 (scope philosophy): three options — big design up front (8-10 entity types), minimum viable (only what evolved Phase 1 needs), hybrid (Phase 1 plus buffer). Chose minimum viable. Captured in DEC-038.

Q3 (methodology choice for CBM redo): two options — original 13-phase methodology (live, currently mid-execution on CBM) or evolved methodology (research-stage at PRDs/process/research/evolved-methodology/, simulator-tested against CBM on 04-30-26). Chose evolved methodology — adoption pilot doubles as v2 real-world test. Captured in DEC-038.

Q4 (multi-tenancy): kickoff forbids fundamental storage architecture changes, putting multi-tenant v2 out of scope. Finding (not a decision): one v2 instance per engagement. CBM gets its own v2 instance; CBM's Mission Statement uses the existing Charter entity (versioned-replace + Make Current pattern) — no new entity type for Mission Statement. Captured in DEC-039.

Q5 (entity inventory): Phase 1 produces Mission Statement (→ Charter), Domain Inventory, Prioritized Backbone, Initial CRM Candidate Set. Initial proposal: three entity types — domain, process, crm_candidate. Doug pushed back that excluding entity while including crm_candidate was strange. Phase 1 interview guide v0.2 line 62 confirms: 'Phase 1 may surface entity names as nouns the client uses, but does not produce Entity PRDs.' Revised: four entity types — domain, entity (thin), process (thin), crm_candidate. Persona deferred (Phase 1 guide explicitly excludes persona elicitation). Captured in DEC-039.

Q6 (per-conversation product): what does each schema-design conversation produce? Three options — (1) design only, (2) design plus build prompt, (3) design plus integration analysis. Chose (1): each schema-design conversation produces a schema spec, the DEC-NNN decisions made during it, and a session record; build prompts come from a separate v0.4-build-planning conversation that takes all four specs as input. Captured in DEC-040.

Q7 (conversation order): domain → entity → process → crm_candidate. Reasoning: domain is foundational; entity is independent of process; process is most relational; crm_candidate is independent coda. Alternative (crm_candidate first as warmup) rejected because warmup-first delays testing the spec methodology against relational complexity. Captured in DEC-040.

Q8 (schema-spec methodology doc outline): nine sections — Identity, Fields, Relationships, Lifecycle, API surface, UI considerations, Acceptance criteria, Open questions, Cross-references. Confirmed.

Q9 (UI considerations section pattern): template-with-deviation-by-justification — default panel layout given, schema may diverge with explicit rationale. Captured in DEC-040.

Q10 (existing v0.4 kickoff supersession): three options — (X) mark in place, (Y) delete, (Z) archive. Chose (X) — supersession header at the top of the existing kickoff; SES-010's artifacts_produced reference remains valid. Captured in DEC-041.

Q11 (PI-001 fourth deferral mechanics): kickoff requires fourth deferral to have new tracking mechanism plus rationale. Three options — (α) simple tracking, (β) CBM-redo-friction trigger, (γ) hard backstop. Chose (β) — if CBM redo Phase 1 surfaces visual friction on any of the four new methodology panels, PI-001 gets pulled to v0.5 ahead of any other v0.5 candidate. Captured in DEC-042.

Q12 (SES-010 identifier-asymmetry resolution): three options — defer entirely, GET helpers for new four only, GET helpers for all 12 prefixed entity types (with PI-002 tracking option C for future). Chose option (3): all 12. Captured in DEC-043.

Final synthesis: deliverables of this conversation confirmed as the workstream plan, schema-spec methodology guide, four per-entity kickoff prompts, supersession edit, six consolidated decisions, one PI patch, four new planning items.

after_payload.alternatives_considered: **Big-design inventory (8-10 entity types in v0.4).** Considered. Rejected per DEC-038's minimum-viable scope philosophy. **Three entity types (domain, process, crm_candidate), entity in v0.5.** Considered. Rejected on Doug's observation that picking CRM candidates without entity vocabulary in v0.4 creates internal incoherence — and the Phase 1 guide's line 62 confirms entity names surface naturally in Phase 1 conversation. **Persona included.** Considered. Rejected because Phase 1 guide explicitly excludes persona elicitation; including persona would deliver a feature that Phase 1 work does not exercise. **Multi-tenant v2 with a client/engagement entity scoping all data.** Considered. Rejected because it crosses the kickoff's no-architecture-changes line and adds complexity not earned by current need (CBM is the only second engagement on the horizon). **Mission Statement as new entity type.** Considered. Persona is not a Phase 1 output and the guide explicitly excludes persona elicitation from Phase 1.
after_payload.decision: v0.4 ships **four new entity types**: ``domain``, ``entity``, ``process``, ``crm_candidate``. **Persona deferred to v0.5+** per PI-003. **Field, requirement, manual-config item, test spec deferred to v0.5+** per PI-004. **Full process schema growth deferred to v0.5+** per PI-005. Persona is *not* analogously included because the Phase 1 interview guide explicitly excludes persona elicitation in Phase 1 — persona context comes from pre-engagement reading of operational role definitions, used as consultant background rather than captured as records. Charter hosting CBM's Mission Statement (rather than a new mission_statement entity) reuses existing versioned-replace + Make Current semantics that fit the 'drafted by CRM Builder, verified by client' Phase 1 pattern exactly.

after_payload.alternatives_considered: **Design + build prompt per schema-design conversation (option 2).** Considered. Rejected because cross-cutting v0.4 concerns benefit from being designed once with all four schemas visible, and because design quality tends to compromise when build delivery is on the table. **Design + integration analysis but no build prompts (option 3).** Considered. Rejected as a middle-ground that adds the integration analysis burden to schema-design conversations without gaining the design-focus benefit of option 1. **Order: crm_candidate first as warmup.** Considered. Rejected because warmup-first delays testing the spec methodology against relational complexity — methodology gaps wouldn't surface until conversation 2 and would retroactively affect the warmup. **No schema-spec methodology guide; each conversation invents its own structure.** Considered. Rejected because inconsistent spec shapes would force the v0.4-build-planning conversation to translate between formats. **No UI template; each schema designs from scratch.** Considered. Rejected because consistent panel patterns are themselves a usability feature, and divergent layouts mean four bespoke build slices instead of four uniform ones.
after_payload.consequences: Six future conversations follow this one: four schema-design conversations (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate) plus a v0.4-build-planning conversation plus a v0.4-build closeout conversation when the slices ship. Each schema-design conversation reads the workstream plan, the spec methodology guide, prior schema specs in the same workstream, and its own per-entity kickoff prompt before opening its first architectural question. The v0.4-build-planning conversation's first task is the cross-spec consistency check defined in section 7.2 of the spec methodology guide.
after_payload.context: DEC-038 and DEC-039 committed v0.4 to a methodology entity schema design workstream covering four entity types under (b-α) path. The workstream's structure — what each schema-design conversation produces, in what order, and how the four specs feed v0.4 build — needed settling before the per-entity kickoff prompts could be authored.
after_payload.decision: Each per-entity schema-design conversation produces **design only**: a schema specification document at ``PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/{entity_type}.md`` conforming to the template in ``methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md``, plus the DEC-NNN decisions made during the conversation, plus a SES-NNN session record written at conversation close. **No build prompts in schema-design conversations.** Build prompts are authored in a single **v0.4-build-planning conversation** that takes all four schema specs as input and produces the v0.4 PRD, implementation plan, and slice build prompts. **Conversation order: domain → entity → process → crm_candidate, sequential, no parallelism.** A **schema-spec methodology guide** at ``methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`` defines the template every schema spec follows; this conversation produces the guide alongside the four per-entity kickoff prompts and the workstream plan. UI considerations in section 3.6 of the spec template use the **template-with-deviation-by-justification** pattern: a default panel layout is given, and a schema may diverge with explicit rationale.
after_payload.rationale: Design-only conversations stay focused on schema design rather than compromising on the design side to ship a build prompt. Separating build planning into its own conversation lets cross-cutting v0.4 concerns (migration sequencing, sidebar ordering, About bump, README, test target, closeout) be designed with all four schemas visible at once rather than scattered across four conversations. The chosen order respects dependencies: domain is foundational (entity and process reference it); entity is independent of process and gets a clean design pass before process complicates the picture; process is the most relational (touches both domain and entity) so designing it third means both referents already exist; crm_candidate is fully independent and acts as a coda. A schema-spec methodology guide produces structural consistency across the four specs — without it, the first schema-design conversation would invent conventions the next three would have to retrofit. Template-with-deviation for UI considerations produces visual consistency across the four new panels and makes the v0.4 build slice prompts simpler, while still allowing per-schema deviation where justified.

after_payload.decision: **PI-001 defers a fourth time with a CBM-redo-friction trigger mechanism.** Rationale: methodology entity schema design takes the v0.4 slot; styling continues to be lower-priority than enabling CBM redo to run on v2 as content system of record. **Trigger mechanism:** if CBM redo Phase 1 (running against the four new methodology entity panels delivered in v0.4) surfaces visual friction on any of those panels, PI-001 gets pulled to v0.5 ahead of any other v0.5 candidate, regardless of v0.5 planning's other priorities. after_payload.alternatives_considered: **(1) Defer entirely — v0.4 ships no helpers, new four inherit documented asymmetry.** Rejected — multiplies the friction by four exactly when CBM redo's direct-API and MCP patterns will hit it most. **(2) (B) for new four only, existing eight remain documented-only.** Rejected — creates inconsistent API surface; consultant expectation breaks. **(C) inline in v0.4 (POST body schema change).** Rejected — gray-area under kickoff constraints, deserves dedicated design pass, tracked as PI-002 instead.
after_payload.consequences: v0.4 build includes ``GET /<plural>/next-identifier`` endpoints for all 12 prefixed entity types (4 new, 8 retrofitted). PI-002 carries forward option (C) for future design.
after_payload.context: SES-010 documented a friction: v2's desktop dialogs auto-assign identifiers via compute_next_*_identifier helpers, hiding identifier computation from the user, but ``POST /<entity>`` requires the identifier in the request body, so direct-API consumers (curl, MCP, scripts) hit ``request_validation_error: body.identifier — Field required`` if they don't compute and supply it. The original v0.4 kickoff named three resolution options: (A) document only (already in place via post-SES-010 CLAUDE.md updates), (B) add ``GET /<entity>/next-identifier`` helper endpoints, (C) make ``identifier`` optional in POST bodies with server-side auto-assignment on omission. v0.4 adds four new prefixed-identifier entity types (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate), each inheriting the asymmetry unless resolved.
after_payload.decision: v0.4 build engages option **(B) for all twelve prefixed-identifier entity types** — the four new ones (DOM, ENT, PROC, CRM working assumptions) plus retroactive helpers on the existing prefixed entity types (decision SES, risk, planning-item, topic, reference, charter version, status version). **Option (C) is tracked as PI-002** — making ``identifier`` optional in POST bodies — as a future ergonomic improvement that needs more design (default-vs-required ambiguity for clients that want to specify identifier). Helper endpoints follow the path convention ``GET /<plural>/next-identifier`` and return the next available identifier for that entity type computed via the existing access-layer pattern.
after_payload.rationale: Option (B) fits the kickoff's 'additive endpoints required by new write surfaces' exception cleanly — new endpoints, no changes to existing endpoint contracts. Retrofitting existing entities matters because inconsistency is its own friction: a consultant who learns ``GET /domains/next-identifier`` works will reasonably expect ``GET /decisions/next-identifier`` to work too, and a 404 is worse UX than no helpers anywhere. Retrofit cost is small (~10 lines per endpoint), small fraction of v0.4 build scope. Option (C) is the cleaner end state but crosses into changing existing endpoint contracts (POST body schema changes), which is gray-area under the kickoff's 'doesn't alter v0.3's storage shape' rule and deserves its own design pass — hence the PI rather than inclusion in v0.4.

Nine architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix. Adopted DOM (3 letters, matches existing v2 governance-entity norm DEC/SES/RSK/TOP/REF/CHR/STA). Affirmed soft-3-letter posture for the workstream rather than locking strict 3 — downstream specs (process, crm_candidate) retain flexibility per spec guide's 3-5 range where ambiguity warrants 4 letters. → DEC-044.

Q2 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted two-content-field split (domain_purpose + domain_description) rather than collapsing to one description field, preserving Phase 1 output shape and separating priority-test artifact from work content. Added optional domain_notes as consultant scratchpad. Skipped mnemonic short_code field (deferred to PI-007); plain text for description (markdown deferred to CBM-redo signal); flat optional notes (structured journal deferred). → DEC-045.

Q3 follow-up — Parent-prefix field-naming convention. User-introduced cross-spec convention change: prefix common/generic fields with parent entity name to prevent confusion. Resolved (a) scope: option iii — applies to fields common across many entity types but not entity-specific by nature, with the realization that all domain v0.4 fields are common-or-could-be-common so all fields are prefixed including identifier and timestamps; (b) retroactivity: applies to existing governance entities too, but retrofit conflicts with (b-α) discipline and is tracked separately as PI-006; (c) identifier and timestamps: also get prefixed. Spec guide section 6 amendment queued for v0.4-build planning. → DEC-046.

Q3 — Status lifecycle. Adopted three values (candidate / confirmed / deferred) with default-candidate starter. Implemented one-way propose-verify gate (no regression from confirmed or deferred back to candidate). Rejection handled via soft-delete rather than a rejected status value; no archived status. Established cross-spec principle: status tracks engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record. → DEC-047.

Q4 — Field-level validation specifics. No storage-level length caps on text fields in v0.4 (UI placeholders provide soft guidance; caps easy to add via migration later). Case-insensitive uniqueness on domain_name within the engagement. Flat plain-text domain_notes (no structured journal in v0.4). → Folded into DEC-045.

Q5 — Relationships. No outgoing relationships from domain in v0.4 (no FKs, no hierarchy, no source-side references-entity use). Inbound relationship kinds entity_scopes_to_domain and process_belongs_to_domain declared in source-side specs (entity.md and process.md), not in domain.md; domain.md lists them informationally in section 3.3.2. Mechanism choice (direct FK vs references-entity edge) deferred to source-side specs. Established {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern for methodology relationship-kind vocab; governance vocab unchanged. → DEC-048.

Q6 — API surface. Adopted standard cross-spec endpoint set with no deviations (8 endpoints including /next-identifier helper per DEC-043). Server-side status-transition validation returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4; additional filters deferred to CBM-redo signal. → Folded into DEC-049.

Q7 — UI considerations. Adopted spec guide default ListDetailPanel layout with no architectural deviations. New 'Methodology' sidebar group below Governance with domain first (entity, process, crm_candidate following in workstream order; all four shipping together in v0.4). Master columns Identifier/Name/Status/Updated. Detail pane with domain_notes under collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default. → Folded into DEC-049.

Q8 — Acceptance criteria. Drafted 14 numbered testable statements covering schema migration, access-layer methods, REST endpoints, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore round-trip, UI structural elements, CRUD round-trip, file-watch refresh, and end-to-end CBM-redo Phase 1 authoring through the dialog. → Folded into DEC-049.

Q9 — Open questions and cross-references. Catalogued three v0.4-build-settle items (spec guide section 6 amendment; source-side mechanism choice for inbound relationships; concurrent identifier-assignment behavior), six CBM-redo-surface items (markdown for description/purpose; storage length caps; structured notes journal; one-way gate friction; archived status; server-side list filters), and five v0.5+ planning items (PI-003, PI-004, PI-005 existing; PI-006 retrofit and PI-007 short_code new this conversation). Consolidated nine question outcomes into six decisions (DEC-044 through DEC-049) rather than nine separate records, mirroring SES-011's consolidation pattern.

PI-008 proposed for the architectural fix: build inbox folder watcher into the v0.3 desktop application so future close-outs require no script execution at all.

after_payload.alternatives_considered: DOMAIN (6 letters, verbose, breaks rhythm with existing prefixes). D (too short, ambiguous). DM (2 letters, breaks 3-5 convention, collision-risk with future entities). Strict 3-letter convention for all methodology entities (rejected as preemptively constraining process and crm_candidate before their conversations open).
after_payload.consequences: domain.md uses DOM-NNN throughout. Downstream entity, process, crm_candidate specs choose their own prefixes with soft-3-letter posture as guidance; each justifies its choice in its own section 3.1. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies no prefix collisions across the four methodology entity types.
after_payload.context: The domain methodology entity needs an identifier prefix. The spec guide allows 3-5 uppercase letters; existing v2 governance entities use 3 letters consistently (DEC, SES, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA; PI is the lone 2-letter outlier predating the convention). domain is the first methodology entity to land in the methodology-entity-schema-design workstream, so its prefix choice carries implicit weight for the downstream three specs (entity, process, crm_candidate).
after_payload.decision: Adopt DOM as the identifier prefix for the domain methodology entity. Format DOM-NNN, zero-padded to 3 digits, server-assigned on POST omission via the GET /domains/next-identifier helper (per DEC-043 / SES-010 resolution). Affirm the v2 norm of 3-letter prefixes without locking it as a strict requirement for downstream methodology entities; the spec guide section 6 range of 3-5 letters remains open for downstream entities (process, crm_candidate) where 3 letters introduces ambiguity (e.g., PROC over PRC for clarity; CRMC over CRM to avoid product-name collision).
after_payload.rationale: DOM is three letters, unambiguous, has no collision risk with any existing prefix, reads naturally as 'domain', and matches the existing v2 pattern visually. The soft-3-letter posture preserves downstream flexibility — locking 3 strict would force PRC over PROC or CRM over CRMC even where the 4-letter form is clearer.
after_payload.title: domain identifier prefix and format

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Collapse purpose into description as the lead sentence (simpler but loses the field-level distinction Phase 1 produces). Make notes structured journal pattern with timestamped entries (ahead-of-need for v0.4; v0.5 candidate). Impose storage-level length caps now (premature constraint without real-use signal). Add mnemonic short_code field for 2-letter domain codes like MN/MR/CR/FU (tracked as PI-007 deferred to v0.5+).
after_payload.consequences: domain.md section 3.2 defines these seven fields with the validation rules above. Phase 1 Domain Inventory content flows into v2 with no field-mapping translation. CBM-redo authoring workflow uses the New Domain dialog to populate purpose and description as distinct fields. PI-007 captures short_code as future work.
after_payload.context: The Phase 1 evolved methodology interview guide (section 7.2) produces a Domain Inventory with one paragraph per domain containing name (in client's language), structural code, one-sentence purpose, and brief description of work covered. The domain schema in v0.4 needs to host this output under minimum-viable scope while leaving room for v0.5+ growth as Phase 3+ iteration work demands.
after_payload.decision: Adopt seven explicit fields plus inherited timestamps for the domain entity in v0.4: domain_identifier (DOM-NNN, server-assigned), domain_name (client's language, case-insensitive unique within engagement), domain_purpose (required, one-sentence, plain text), domain_description (required, brief paragraph, plain text), domain_notes (optional, plain text consultant scratchpad not part of client-facing Domain Inventory render), domain_status (see DEC-047), plus inherited domain_created_at, domain_updated_at, domain_deleted_at. No storage-level length caps in v0.4; UI placeholder text provides soft guidance. Markdown rendering on description and purpose deferred to CBM-redo signal.
after_payload.rationale: Splitting purpose and description (rather than collapsing to one description field) preserves the Phase 1 output shape 1:1 with no data loss on the way in, separates the why (purpose, priority-test artifact) from the what (description, work content), and enables differentiated UI rendering. Optional notes captures consultant rationale (pattern-library reasoning, push-back trails) without conflating with client-facing content. No length caps because caps are easy to add via migration later and hard to relax once imposed; CBM redo will surface whether pathological inputs justify them. Case-insensitive name uniqueness within engagement prevents duplicate-domain confusion in v2's one-instance-per-engagement model.
after_payload.title: domain field inventory and validation under minimum-viable v0.4 scope

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Keep bare status, name, description per spec guide section 6 (preserves existing v2 norm but accepts the ambiguity as the entity count grows). Apply prefix only to enum/classification fields like status, leaving name/description bare (rejected as inconsistent — partial coverage invites reinterpretation). Apply prefix to all fields uniformly including those entity-specific by nature (rejected as overreaching when entity-specific fields are unambiguous on their own).
after_payload.consequences: All four methodology workstream specs (domain.md, entity.md, process.md, crm_candidate.md) use parent-prefix field naming. The spec guide section 6 needs amendment to reflect the new convention — flagged for the v0.4-build-planning conversation. Governance-entity retrofit (PI-006) becomes a v0.4-build-planning sizing question (pull into v0.4 scope or defer to v0.5+). The methodology/governance field-naming split signals 'this is methodology, this is governance' as a useful structural tell until PI-006 closes the gap.
after_payload.context: Generic field names like status, name, description appearing on multiple entity types create reference ambiguity when fields are discussed across entities (which status? which name?). The spec guide section 6 currently mandates bare status as the cross-spec convention, mirroring existing v2 governance entities (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA). The domain schema-design conversation surfaced this as a friction point when establishing conventions that the next three methodology specs (entity, process, crm_candidate) will inherit.
after_payload.decision: Adopt parent-prefix field naming for all four methodology entities in this workstream: all non-identifier and non-timestamp fields are prefixed with the parent entity name. For domain, this produces domain_name, domain_purpose, domain_description, domain_notes, domain_status. Identifier and timestamps also adopt the prefix for full convention consistency: domain_identifier, domain_created_at, domain_updated_at, domain_deleted_at. Convention scope: applies to common-or-could-be-common fields; fields that are genuinely entity-specific in nature would stay bare, though for domain v0.4 every field is common-or-could-be-common so all fields are prefixed. The convention is forward-only for the methodology workstream; governance-entity retrofit is tracked as PI-006.
after_payload.rationale: The prefix disambiguates field references in cross-spec discussions (entity_status vs process_status vs domain_status is unambiguous; bare status requires context). The convention costs little for new entities — naming is a sunk cost paid once at design — and the value compounds as the entity count grows. Forward-only posture for methodology is safe because methodology entities are new in v0.4; retrofitting governance entities is a substantial migration that needs its own scoping (PI-006).
after_payload.title: Parent-prefix field-naming convention for methodology entities (cross-spec rule)

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Add rejected status value (rejected; duplicates soft-delete which already supports the show-deleted toggle and restore endpoint). Add archived status value (rejected; same duplication concern). Allow backflow to candidate (rejected; conflates engagement-scope state with deliberation state). Two-value lifecycle candidate/confirmed only (rejected; loses the deferred-but-real-domain distinction that Phase 1 needs at domain granularity, mirroring the process-level deferred-list pattern).
after_payload.consequences: domain.md section 3.4 specifies the three values, the transition matrix, and the soft-delete-handles-rejection posture. Server-side transition validation returns HTTP 422 on invalid transitions with an invalid_status_transition error body. Cross-spec consequence: downstream methodology specs adopt the candidate starter and the rejection-via-soft-delete posture unless they document a substantive deviation. CBM redo will surface whether the one-way gate creates friction; if so, v0.5 transition-map amendment opens regression paths.
after_payload.context: Phase 1 of the evolved methodology has CRM Builder proposing domains and the client verifying them (Principle 4 — CRM Builder proposes, client verifies). The domain entity needs a status lifecycle that captures this engagement-scope flow without conflating engagement-scope state with deliberation state or with record-existence state.
after_payload.decision: Adopt three status values for domain: candidate (default starter; CRM Builder proposed, awaiting client verification), confirmed (client-verified, in scope for the engagement), deferred (client-acknowledged real domain but out of current engagement scope). Implement a one-way propose-verify gate: candidate transitions to confirmed or deferred but not back to candidate from either; confirmed and deferred transition between each other freely. Rejection handled via soft-delete (DELETE sets domain_deleted_at; restorable via POST /restore) rather than a rejected status value. No archived status — soft-delete already covers retained-for-record-not-in-scope.
after_payload.rationale: Three values cover the propose-verify happy path plus the acknowledged-but-not-now case without inventing values that duplicate soft-delete. The one-way gate makes propose-verify a meaningful engagement moment — if a consultant wants to fundamentally rethink a verified domain, the right action is editing content, not regressing status. The principle 'status tracks engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record' is cross-spec applicable and downstream methodology specs adopt it unless they have substantive reason to deviate. The default-candidate starter affirms the spec guide section 6 'typical for evolving methodology entities' note.
after_payload.title: domain status lifecycle, propose-verify gate, and rejection-via-soft-delete posture

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Target-side declaration in domain.md (rejected; puts downstream-dependency declarations in upstream specs, inverts source-side ownership). domain.md commits authoritative mechanism choice for inbound edges (rejected; the choice belongs to source-side specs, and the cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning is the appropriate gate). verb_phrase-only relationship-kind naming with no source prefix (rejected for methodology vocab because of collision risk; retained for governance vocab as forward-only posture).
after_payload.consequences: domain.md section 3.3 is target-side-observer-only. entity.md and process.md make the authoritative mechanism choices for entity_scopes_to_domain and process_belongs_to_domain respectively. The v0.4-build-planning conversation's cross-spec consistency check validates that the source-side mechanism choices align. Spec guide section 6 needs amendment to reflect the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern for methodology vocab — flagged with the field-naming convention amendment under coordination with PI-006 at v0.4-build-planning. Existing governance vocab vocab.py entries (cites, supersedes) unchanged.
after_payload.context: domain is the foundational methodology entity that entity and process will reference. The domain schema-design conversation needs to settle (a) what relationships domain itself declares versus what its inbound targets declare, (b) what mechanism (direct FK vs references-entity edge vs hierarchy) those inbound relationships use, and (c) what naming convention applies to relationship-kind vocabulary entries involving methodology entities.
after_payload.decision: domain has no outgoing relationships in v0.4: no FK fields to other entity types, no self-referential hierarchy, no source-side use of the references entity. Inbound relationships from entity and process are declared in those specs (source-side declaration, not target-side). Anticipated inbound kinds are listed informationally in domain.md section 3.3.2 (entity_scopes_to_domain via references-entity edge for many-to-many; process_belongs_to_domain via direct FK for many-to-one) but actual vocab.py registration and mechanism choice belong to entity.md and process.md. Cross-spec naming convention established here: relationship-kind values involving methodology entities use {source}_{verb}_{target} pattern (source entity name, verb phrase, target entity name) — e.g., entity_scopes_to_domain, process_belongs_to_domain. Forward-only for methodology vocab; governance vocabulary (cites, supersedes, etc.) retains its existing verb-phrase-only pattern.
after_payload.rationale: Source-side declaration matches normal RDBMS modeling (the edge lives where the source is) and the verb_phrase reads source-first anyway (entity_scopes_to_domain — entity is the subject). The methodology workstream has multiple entities with similar verbs (entity_belongs_to_domain could collide with process_belongs_to_domain without source-prefixing), so prefixing the source disambiguates direction at read time and prevents collisions. No outgoing relationships in v0.4 reflects the explicit out-of-scope items in the workstream plan section 3.1 (no sub-domain hierarchy, no Cross-Domain Service distinction) and keeps the v0.4 schema thin.
after_payload.title: domain relationship posture and {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming convention

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Add server-side ?domain_status=... filter to the list endpoint (rejected for v0.4; client-side filtering over 3-8 records is sufficient). Render domain_notes always-visible in the detail pane (rejected; collapsed-by-default's visual cue is cheap and reinforces 'internal' semantics). Different sidebar group label such as 'Implementation Content' or 'Client Content' (rejected; 'Methodology' is already established in workstream documents and is the cleanest distinction from 'Governance'). Fewer than 10 acceptance criteria (rejected; spec guide section 7.1 requires at least 10).
after_payload.consequences: domain.md sections 3.5 (API), 3.6 (UI), 3.7 (acceptance criteria) lock these specifications. v0.4 build planning translates the 14 acceptance criteria into specific test cases. The Methodology sidebar group is introduced as a single shipping unit in v0.4 containing all four methodology entity panels.
after_payload.context: With identifier, fields, relationships, and lifecycle settled, the remaining v0.4 specifications for domain are the REST endpoint set, server-side validation behaviors, the desktop UI layout, and the testable acceptance criteria for build planning.
after_payload.decision: Adopt the cross-spec default REST endpoint set with no deviations: GET /domains, GET /domains/{id}, POST /domains, PUT /domains/{id}, PATCH /domains/{id}, DELETE /domains/{id}, POST /domains/{id}/restore, GET /domains/next-identifier. Status-transition validation enforced server-side at the access layer returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body on illegal transitions. List endpoint supports only ?include_deleted=true in v0.4; additional filters deferred to CBM-redo signal. Adopt the spec guide default ListDetailPanel UI layout with no architectural deviations: new 'Methodology' sidebar group below 'Governance' (with domain first, entity/process/crm_candidate following in workstream order, all shipping together in v0.4); master pane columns Identifier/Name/Status/Updated sorted ascending by identifier; right-click context menu New/Edit/Delete/Restore per DEC-035 and DEC-036; detail pane fields in section 3.2 order with domain_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default; CRUD dialogs via EntityCrudDialog subclasses. Acceptance criteria captured as 14 numbered testable statements covering schema migration, access-layer methods, REST endpoints, identifier auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore round-trip, UI structural elements, CRUD round-trip, file-watch refresh, and end-to-end CBM-redo Phase 1 authoring through the dialog.
after_payload.rationale: No-deviation default endpoints minimize cross-spec complexity and reuse existing v2 patterns. Server-side transition validation prevents UI-only enforcement bugs from corrupting the engagement-scope state. Single ?include_deleted flag is the minimum that lets the show-deleted UI toggle work; further filters are speculative without real-use signal. Default UI layout fits domain's small-record-count, scalar-field, no-outgoing-relationship shape exactly — designing a custom layout would invent complexity without benefit. domain_notes collapsed-by-default reinforces field semantics (internal scratchpad, not client-facing) through UI affordance. Fourteen acceptance criteria covers the spec guide's ten typical categories with appropriate depth without over-fragmenting.
after_payload.title: domain API surface, UI defaults, and acceptance criteria for v0.4

after_payload.description: DEC-046 establishes the parent-prefix field-naming convention for methodology entities forward-only; governance entities (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA) retain bare field names (status, title, name, description, etc.) until this retrofit lands. Retrofit scope: Alembic migrations renaming columns on eight existing tables; access-layer method updates across crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/; REST API serialization updates; MCP tool input/output updates; UI dialog updates (every QLineEdit, QComboBox, and table model column bound to a renamed field); DB-export JSON snapshot regeneration with rewriting of committed snapshots in git history; backward-compat considerations for any external scripts hitting the REST API or MCP. Substantial migration. The v0.4-build-planning conversation decides whether to pull this retrofit into v0.4 scope (significant additional work) or defer to v0.5+. The methodology workstream's specs ship with the new convention regardless of when this retrofit lands.
after_payload.title: Retrofit governance entities to parent-prefix field-naming convention

after_payload.description: The existing CBM methodology uses 2-letter domain codes (MN for Mentoring, MR for Mentor Recruitment, CR for Client Recruiting, FU for Fundraising) as prefixes for process and entity identifiers (MN-INTAKE, MR-RECRUIT, etc.). The Phase 1 evolved-methodology interview guide section 7.2 says the Domain Inventory includes 'name in client's language and a structural code' but does not specify whether downstream process/entity codes continue to use mnemonic prefixes in the evolved methodology. DEC-045 defers domain_short_code from the v0.4 minimum-viable shape pending CBM-redo signal. If real-engagement Phase 3 work confirms that mnemonic prefixes still pull weight for human-readable process/entity identifier construction, a v0.5 schema migration adds domain_short_code (str, 2-4 uppercase letters, unique within engagement); the process and entity schemas adopt it for human-readable identifier prefixes alongside their numeric identifiers. If the evolved methodology converges on sequential identifiers without mnemonic prefixes, this PI closes as not-needed.
after_payload.title: domain.short_code field for mnemonic references and downstream identifier prefixes

after_payload.description: Surfaced by SES-012 close-out friction. The current pattern requires Doug to either (a) copy-paste session content into the v0.3 desktop New Session dialog and run a per-conversation apply script for decisions/PIs/references, or (b) run a generic apply_close_out.py script that processes a JSON payload file (mid-SES-012 mitigation). Both still require a manual command after each schema-design conversation. The architectural fix: build an inbox folder watcher into the v0.3 desktop application. The app monitors a known directory (e.g., crmbuilder-v2/inbox/ or PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/), detects new JSON payload files, prompts the user to review (showing a summary of what would be written), and on confirmation imports all records via the existing access layer. Processed files move to inbox/processed/ for traceability. Idempotent on re-run (HTTP 409 = already present, skip). Implementation scope: file-watch infrastructure (already exists for db-export refresh — possibly reusable), payload-schema validation, summary-and-confirm modal, error reporting per record. The v0.4-build-planning conversation decides whether to pull this into v0.4 scope (eliminates close-out friction for all four workstream conversations) or defer to v0.5+. If deferred, the apply_close_out.py script remains the close-out pattern.
after_payload.title: Inbox folder watcher in v0.3 desktop app for close-out JSON payloads

after_payload.summary: Second of four schema-design conversations in the methodology entity schema-design workstream. Produced the entity schema specification (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md, 452 lines) defining the entity entity type for v2 storage under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Eight architectural questions resolved into five decisions (DEC-050 through DEC-054). Inherited and applied without re-decision the two cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048). Registered entity_scopes_to_domain as the first formal cross-entity vocabulary entry in the methodology workstream — many-to-many via the universal references table. Two new planning items authored (PI-009 master-pane Domains column on Entities panel paired with PI-007 short codes; PI-010 entity-schema v0.5+ extensions covering variants and base-type/kind classification). Successor conversation: process schema design.
after_payload.title: entity schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #2
after_payload.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `entity` entity type schema for v2's storage layer. The 'entity' here is the CRM-modeled noun — Contact, Account, Session, Engagement, Dues — the things the eventual CRM stores records about. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md` — the complete schema specification per the template in `methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`. Ship thin in v0.4 — name, brief description, status — designed with growth path in mind for v0.5+ when Phase 3 demands attach fields, relationships, and full PRD content."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root, including the v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master, noting line 168 names ENT-NNN as the working assumption per DEC-043); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (predecessor spec, 379 lines, source of inherited conventions); phase-1-interview-guide.md v0.2 (line 62 entity-definition scope; section 7.2 Domain Inventory output spec excluding candidate entities; section 4.3 between-sessions discipline); close-out-payloads/ses_012.json (DEC-044..049 + PI-006/007/008 from the domain conversation; payload exists but db-export snapshots through SES-011/DEC-043 confirm it has not yet been applied — numbers in this payload assume SES-012 lands first); decisions.json snapshot (DEC-038/039/043 read in detail for context on derived-fields, minimum-viable inventory rationale, identifier-asymmetry resolution).

Eight architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix and format. Affirmed ENT under domain.md's soft-3-letter posture (DEC-044). Three letters, unambiguous, no collision with existing prefixes (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM), matches v2 governance-entity norm, already named as the working assumption in DEC-043 and workstream plan line 168. → DEC-050.

Q2 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted a single content field (entity_description) rather than mirroring domain's two-content-field split (purpose + description). Reasoning: domain_purpose answers a priority-test mission question that has no analog for entity — an entity is a noun the client uses, and the 'why this entity matters to the engagement' question is answered relationally by its entity_scopes_to_domain references, not by free-text purpose. Adding entity_purpose would duplicate entity_description or invent a methodology concept Phase 1 doesn't produce. Five substantive fields plus inherited timestamps: entity_identifier, entity_name, entity_description, entity_notes (optional), entity_status. No entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (Phase 3 territory; deferred under PI-010). No storage-level length caps (UI soft guidance only, matching domain). Case-insensitive entity_name uniqueness within the engagement. Parent-prefix field naming inherited from DEC-046 without re-establishment. → DEC-051.

Q3 — Status lifecycle. Adopted domain's three-status pattern unchanged (candidate / confirmed / deferred), one-way propose-verify gate, freely bidirectional between confirmed and deferred, rejection via soft-delete, no archived status. Documented the entity-specific status-independence-from-affiliation-status posture: an entity's entity_status is its own field, set by the consultant based on client verification of the entity itself, not derived from the statuses of the domains the entity scopes to. This independence matters because an entity may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions (Contact in CBM might scope to MN/MR confirmed and FU deferred simultaneously, and Contact's own status is confirmed because the client agreed Contact is a thing in their world). Server-side transition validation returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body, identical to domain. Noted as a future v0.5+ open question: a separate 'defined' or 'specified' status may surface once fields land, or the references-table cardinality between entity and field may suffice as the implicit signal. → DEC-052.

Q4 — Domain affiliation mechanism. The architecturally consequential question. Considered three candidates: (A) references-entity edge with new entity_scopes_to_domain relationship kind, (B) multi-value FK column on entity table, (C) direct single-value FK. (C) rejected outright — contradicts the methodology reality that entities span domains (CBM Contact lives in MN/MR/FU simultaneously). (B) considered but rejected — breaks discipline with v2's references-first posture (DEC-006), reverse queries require scanning all entity rows, FK validation reinvents what the references mechanism handles, and future relationship kinds on entity (entity-touches-process, entity-has-field, entity-has-variant) would each need their own multi-value columns, proliferating schema. Adopted (A). Many-to-many cardinality with no upper bound on either side. Zero-affiliation explicitly permitted (Phase 1 sometimes surfaces an entity name before its domain scoping is settled; the references discipline must not force pre-decided affiliation). Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete references; restore restores them in place. The existing ReferencesSection widget renders the affiliations in the detail pane for free. → DEC-053 (combines mechanism and vocabulary registration since they are inseparable architectural choices).

Q5 — Relationship-kind vocabulary additions. Single new vocab entry: entity_scopes_to_domain (source=entity, target=domain). Conforms to the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern inherited from DEC-048 — naming convention adopted not re-decided. Three coordinated mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: add entry to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS in vocab.py; extend _kinds_for_pair so (entity, domain) returns {entity_scopes_to_domain}; Alembic migration extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint to include the new value. No other vocab additions in this spec; inbound kinds (process_touches_entity, field_belongs_to_entity, entity_variant_of_entity) are anticipated and documented informationally in section 3.3.2 but declared by their source-side specs (process.md and future v0.5+ specs), not by this one. → Folded into DEC-053.

Q6 — API surface. Adopted standard cross-spec endpoint set with no deviations (8 endpoints including /entities/next-identifier helper per DEC-043). Server-side status-transition validation identical to domain's pattern. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4. Decomposed reference handling explicitly called out: domain affiliations are NOT inlined into entity create/update bodies; the client makes separate POST /references calls with source/target/kind. This keeps the entity API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006) and matches how the v0.3 UI handles references for governance entities; the New dialog hides the two-call sequence behind a single user gesture but the API stays decomposed. No shortcut endpoints. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q7 — UI considerations. Adopted spec guide default ListDetailPanel layout with no architectural deviations. Methodology sidebar group position #2 (after Domains, before Processes and CRM Candidates which ship together in v0.4). Standard four-column master pane (identifier, name, status, updated). One real deferral: a Domains-column on the master pane was considered for v0.4 since entity-to-domain affiliation is the entity's defining classification, but deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes. Without short codes, the column would render DOM-001/002/003 — identifiers that mean nothing at a glance; with short codes (MN/MR/FU), the column becomes high-value. The N+1 / batched-join query design isn't free for a column whose value the detail pane already exposes one click away via ReferencesSection. Tracked as new PI-009. Detail pane shows fields in section-3.2 order with entity_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default (mirrors domain). Create dialog's domain-affiliation flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as an open question for v0.4-build planning to decide since both satisfy the acceptance criterion that the user can attach affiliations without leaving the UI. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q8 — Acceptance criteria. Drafted 16 numbered testable statements: 13 mirror domain's structural set (schema migration, identifier format constraint, name uniqueness, status enum/transition validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoint round-trip, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore, sidebar entry, master pane columns, detail pane fields, CRUD dialogs, file-watch refresh) with entity field-name substitutions, plus three specifically covering the new vocabulary registration and references mechanism: (14) entity_scopes_to_domain registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and constrained correctly at _kinds_for_pair and refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint; (15) references created and queryable bidirectionally with persistence across soft-delete/restore of either endpoint; (16) sample CBM-redo Phase 1 records authored through the UI including domain affiliations. → Folded into DEC-054.

Outline approved and end-to-end drafting executed without per-section confirmation. Spec committed to main at 40e2cf5. Consolidated eight question outcomes into five decisions (DEC-050 through DEC-054) — narrower than domain's six because the cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048) carry forward unchanged and are adopted not re-decided in this spec.

Two new planning items authored: PI-009 master-pane Domains column on Entities panel (jointly enabled by PI-007 short codes); PI-010 entity-schema v0.5+ extensions covering variants (Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern; mechanism TBD) and base-type/kind classification (person / organization / event / transaction). Tracked separately from PI-004 (which groups other deferred entity types) because these are entity-schema-growth, not new entity types.

after_payload.alternatives_considered: ENTITY (6 letters, verbose, breaks rhythm with existing prefixes). E (too short, ambiguous). EN (2 letters, breaks 3-5 convention). MENT for 'methodology entity' (verbose, unnecessary disambiguation since 'entity' is unambiguous in this context).
after_payload.consequences: entity.md uses ENT-NNN throughout. No collision with existing prefixes. Downstream process and crm_candidate specs retain the soft-3-letter flexibility preserved by domain.md; each justifies its choice in its own section 3.1. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies no prefix collisions across the four methodology entity types.
after_payload.context: The entity methodology entity needs an identifier prefix. The workstream plan line 168 and DEC-043 both name ENT-NNN as the working assumption. The predecessor domain.md established a soft-3-letter prefix posture: the v2 governance-entity norm is 3 letters (DEC, SES, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA) and domain adopted DOM in keeping with that norm, but the spec guide allows 3-5 letters and downstream specs retain flexibility where 3 letters is ambiguous (e.g., PROC over PRC for clarity; CRMC over CRM to avoid product-name collision).
after_payload.decision: Adopt ENT as the identifier prefix for the entity methodology entity. Format ENT-NNN, zero-padded to 3 digits, server-assigned on POST omission via the GET /entities/next-identifier helper (per DEC-043 / SES-010 resolution).
after_payload.rationale: ENT is three letters, unambiguous, reads naturally as 'entity', has no collision with any existing prefix (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM), and matches the existing v2 pattern visually. Adheres to domain.md's soft-3-letter posture without consuming any of the flexibility-budget the posture preserves for process and crm_candidate.
after_payload.title: entity identifier prefix and format

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Mirror domain's purpose+description split for cross-spec consistency (rejected as forcing a methodology concept that Phase 1 doesn't produce). Include entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (rejected as Phase 3 territory; tracked under PI-010). Storage-level length caps at v0.4 (rejected; caps are easy to add later and hard to relax once imposed; CBM-redo signal preferred for sizing). Markdown for entity_description (deferred to CBM-redo signal; matches domain's posture). Structured-journal pattern for entity_notes (deferred to CBM-redo signal; matches domain_notes posture).
after_payload.consequences: Eight-column entities table. CBM redo's Phase 1 captures entity records with the field set as specified; if real use surfaces methodology gaps (entity_purpose-shaped need, base-type classification need, length pathologies, markdown rendering pressure), they become v0.5+ candidates with concrete real-use evidence. Cross-spec naming convention from DEC-046 applied uniformly: even entity_identifier and timestamps carry the entity_ prefix for full convention consistency.
after_payload.context: DEC-039 explicitly justifies entity's inclusion in v0.4's minimum-viable inventory despite Phase 1 not formally producing an Entity Inventory output: 'real Phase 1 conversations naturally surface entity vocabulary (per the interview guide's line 62) and if v2 has no entity type those names land as loose nouns in session notes or process descriptions, forcing retrofit at the Phase 3 boundary.' DEC-039 names the working minimum as 'name, description, status'. The predecessor domain.md went further, splitting its content side into domain_purpose (the why — priority-test artifact) and domain_description (the what — kinds of work covered) plus optional domain_notes. The question is whether entity warrants the same split or simplifies to one description field.
after_payload.decision: Adopt a five-field shape under the parent-prefix convention inherited from DEC-046: entity_identifier (TEXT, server-assigned, ^ENT-\d{3}$, unique), entity_name (TEXT, required, non-empty trimmed, case-insensitive unique within the engagement), entity_description (TEXT, required, non-empty trimmed, single content field rather than purpose+description split), entity_notes (TEXT, optional, plain text, internal consultant scratchpad), entity_status (TEXT, required, default candidate, enum). Plus inherited timestamp fields (entity_created_at, entity_updated_at, entity_deleted_at) — eight columns total. No entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (deferred under PI-010). No storage-level length caps; UI placeholder text provides soft guidance only.
after_payload.rationale: Single entity_description rather than a purpose+description split because domain_purpose exists to capture the priority-test mission-relationship at domain granularity ('why does the mission require this domain?'), and entity has no comparable mission relationship. An entity is a noun the client uses; the 'why this entity matters to the engagement' question is answered relationally by the entity_scopes_to_domain references, not by free-text purpose. Adding entity_purpose would either duplicate entity_description or invent a methodology concept Phase 1 doesn't produce. entity_notes captures the same consultant-scratchpad role as domain_notes — pattern-library rationale, push-back trails, between-session reasoning. No length caps because caps are easy to add via migration later and hard to relax once imposed; UI soft guidance handles the typical case. No entity_kind because base-type semantics are Phase 3 / CRM-engine territory; v0.4's job is to capture Phase 1 surfacing, not to model CRM implementation specifics.
after_payload.title: entity field inventory and validation under minimum-viable v0.4 scope

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Add a fourth status value 'defined' or 'specified' to distinguish 'client agreed entity is in scope' from 'we have a full definition of this entity' (rejected for v0.4; v0.4 has no fields and the distinction has no expressive shape; revisited as a CBM-redo open question for v0.5+). Derive entity_status from the union of its affiliated-domain statuses (rejected as documented in section 3.4.3). Different transition rules from domain's (no reason to deviate surfaced).
after_payload.consequences: entity.md section 3.4 mirrors domain.md section 3.4 structurally with one additional subsection (3.4.3) on status independence. Cross-spec principle 'status tracks engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record' applied consistently across domain and entity. CBM redo will surface whether the 'defined vs confirmed' distinction is needed for v0.5+; if so, either a fourth status value or the implicit signal from entity-to-field references' presence becomes the mechanism.
after_payload.context: DEC-047 established for domain a three-value lifecycle (candidate / confirmed / deferred) with default candidate starter, one-way propose-verify gate (no regression from confirmed or deferred back to candidate), bidirectional movement between confirmed and deferred, rejection via soft-delete, no archived status. The cross-spec principle 'status values track engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record' was established. The question for entity is whether to mirror domain's lifecycle exactly or deviate, and whether entity-specific status semantics need additional documentation.
after_payload.decision: Adopt domain's three-value lifecycle unchanged. entity_status enum values candidate / confirmed / deferred with identical predecessor/successor transition rules. Default candidate on POST omission; explicit non-starter values permitted on POST for bulk-import scenarios. Server-side transition validation at the access layer returns HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition envelope on bad transitions. Rejection via soft-delete; no archived status. Add one entity-specific subsection (3.4.3) documenting that entity_status is independent of any affiliated-domain statuses — an entity may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions and its own status is set by consultant judgment based on client verification of the entity itself, not derived from references.
after_payload.rationale: Entities, like domains, are surfaced by the consultant and verified by the client; the propose-verify dynamic is the same, so the lifecycle pattern transfers without modification. The status-independence-from-affiliation-status subsection matters because the temptation to derive entity status from the union of its domain affiliations' statuses is real but wrong: Contact in CBM might scope to MN (confirmed), MR (confirmed), FU (deferred) simultaneously, and Contact's own status is confirmed because the client agreed Contact is a thing in their world. Coupling the two would hide that client decision behind a derivation rule. Explicit documentation prevents either (a) future maintainers from introducing the derivation, or (b) downstream specs from inferring different semantics.
after_payload.title: entity status lifecycle (adopts domain pattern; documents status-independent-of-affiliation-status posture)

after_payload.alternatives_considered: Option B (multi-value FK column on entity, e.g., entity_domain_identifiers as JSON array). Option C (direct single-value entity_domain_identifier FK). Hybrid pattern with one 'primary domain' FK plus references for additional affiliations (rejected as inventing complexity without value — CBM's Contact doesn't have a primary domain, it's genuinely shared). Different relationship-kind naming (e.g., target-first 'domain_contains_entity', or generic 'scopes_to'; rejected per DEC-048's source-first convention and disambiguation benefit of explicit source/target naming).
after_payload.consequences: Refs table grows by the count of entity-to-domain edges across the engagement. ReferencesSection widget renders affiliations in the entity detail pane for free; same widget renders inbound entity references from the domain side. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build planning verifies the source-side mechanism choices align across the four methodology entity types. The choice establishes a pattern for downstream specs: when a methodology entity has a many-to-many relationship to another methodology entity, the references-entity edge is the default mechanism unless there's a substantive reason to deviate.
after_payload.context: domain.md section 3.3.2 named many-to-many via the references entity as the working assumption for entity-to-domain affiliation, citing CBM's Contact-shared-across-MN/MR/FU pattern. The mechanism choice belongs to entity.md as the source-side spec. Three candidate mechanisms exist in v2: (A) references-entity edge with new entity_scopes_to_domain relationship kind, (B) multi-value FK column on the entity table, (C) direct single-value FK on the entity table. The references infrastructure (DEC-006) is the established cross-entity-type edge store; the question is whether to use it for entity-to-domain or to introduce a new pattern.
after_payload.decision: Adopt option A: references-entity edge with new relationship kind entity_scopes_to_domain. Mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: (1) add entity_scopes_to_domain to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS in vocab.py; (2) extend _kinds_for_pair so (entity, domain) returns {entity_scopes_to_domain} (the only kind for this pair in v0.4); (3) Alembic migration extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint to include the new value. Many-to-many cardinality with no upper bound on either side. Zero-affiliation explicitly permitted in v0.4. Source must be a live entity record; target must be a live domain record (existing access-layer rules). Duplicate (source_id, target_id, relationship_kind) tuples rejected by the references-table uniqueness constraint. Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete references; restore restores them in place. Vocabulary entry conforms to the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern inherited from DEC-048.
after_payload.rationale: Option C (direct single-value FK) rejected outright — contradicts the methodology reality that entities span domains. Option B (multi-value FK on entity table) considered but rejected — breaks discipline with v2's references-first posture (DEC-006), reverse queries ('what entities scope to this domain?') require scanning all entity rows, FK validation reinvents what the references mechanism already handles, and future relationship kinds on entity (entity-touches-process, entity-has-field, entity-has-variant) would each need their own multi-value columns, proliferating schema for what the references entity exists to absorb. Option A reuses existing v2 infrastructure (refs table, vocab.py, Alembic migration pattern, ReferencesSection widget) and matches how the v0.3 desktop UI handles references for governance entities. Zero-affiliation permitted because Phase 1 sometimes surfaces entity names before domain scoping is settled and the references discipline must not force pre-decided affiliation.
after_payload.title: entity-to-domain affiliation mechanism via references-entity edge + entity_scopes_to_domain vocabulary registration

after_payload.decision: Adopt standard 8-endpoint API surface with no deviations: GET /entities, GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /entities/{id}, POST /entities, POST /entities/{id}/restore, GET /entities/next-identifier. Server-side status-transition validation per the domain pattern. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4. Decomposed reference handling: domain affiliations NOT inlined into entity create/update bodies; client makes separate POST /references calls. Default ListDetailPanel UI in the existing Methodology sidebar group (position #2 after Domains). Standard four-column master pane (identifier, name, status, updated) — defer a Domains column to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes, tracked as new PI-009. Detail pane shows fields in section-3.2 order with entity_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default (mirrors domain). Edge-text confirmation on delete. Create-dialog domain-affiliation flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as an open question for the v0.4-build-planning conversation to decide. 16 testable acceptance criteria covering schema migration, identifier format constraint, name uniqueness, status enum/transition validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoint round-trip, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore, sidebar entry, master pane columns, detail pane fields, CRUD dialogs, file-watch refresh, plus three specific to the new vocabulary registration and references mechanism.
after_payload.rationale: No-deviation default endpoints minimize cross-spec complexity and reuse existing v2 patterns. Decomposed reference handling keeps the entity API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006); shortcut endpoints would invent API surface area that the references-table API already covers. Domains column deferred to v0.5+ for two reasons: (a) without PI-007 short codes, the column would render DOM-001/002/003 — identifiers that mean nothing at a glance, so the column's UX value is tied to PI-007 landing; (b) the batched-join query design isn't free for a column whose value the detail pane already exposes one click away via ReferencesSection. Shipping the column alongside PI-007 keeps both deliverables aligned. after_payload.description: DEC-054 defers a Domains column on the master pane of the Entities panel to v0.5+, jointly enabled by PI-007 (domain.short_code field). The column renders affiliated domains as comma-separated short codes (e.g., 'MN, MR, FU') once short codes exist; without them, DOM-NNN identifiers don't tell a consultant anything at a glance and the column's UX value is lost. Implementation scope when both PI-007 and PI-009 land together: (a) access-layer batched-join query (list_entities_with_affiliations() or equivalent) returning each entity's affiliated-domain short codes alongside its scalar fields without N+1 queries; (b) ListDetailPanel view-model update to surface the new column; (c) column-width and sorting behavior decisions (sort by what? probably alphabetical-by-short-code-list with secondary sort by entity_name). Prioritization signal: CBM redo will reveal whether the absence of the column in v0.4 is high-friction (do consultants frequently click into the detail pane to check affiliations? do they request a list view grouped by domain?). If high-friction, PI-009 + PI-007 move up in v0.5 priority; if low-friction, they stay queued.

after_payload.description: DEC-051 and DEC-053 jointly defer two entity-schema growth areas to v0.5+: (a) entity variants — the Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern from CBM history where two related-but-distinct entities share a base type but carry different fields and role semantics; (b) entity base-type / kind classification — person / organization / event / transaction-style categorization that informs CRM-engine selection and field-shape defaults. Both are intentionally absent from v0.4: variants because Phase 1 doesn't produce them and v0.4 has no fields to vary across them; base-type/kind because it's Phase 3 territory tied to CRM-engine specifics that v0.4's thin shape stays neutral about. Tracked separately from PI-004 (which groups field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec as new methodology entity types) because PI-010 is entity-schema growth — extending the existing entity type — not introducing new entity types. Variant mechanism TBD: candidates include self-referential FK (entity_parent_identifier), references-entity edge (entity_variant_of_entity), or a separate variant entity type sitting between base entity and field. Mechanism choice is a v0.5+ design conversation. Workaround in v0.4: variants are distinguished by suffixing entity_name ('Contact — Mentor', 'Contact — Client'); CBM redo will reveal how much friction the workaround creates and inform mechanism design. Base-type/kind classification: candidate v0.5+ shape is an enum field (entity_kind: person | organization | event | transaction | other) on the entity table, with validation that informs Phase 3 field-shape defaults and Phase 5 CRM-evaluation scoring.
